title: centaurea × aschersoniana
intro: Centaurea × aschersoniana, also known as Aschen's knapweed, is a hybrid plant species in the family Asteraceae, resulting from the cross between Centaurea cyanus and Centaurea scabiosa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic qualities, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Centaurea × aschersoniana include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of this plant are typically harvested and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
